headlines.title: California governor candidates address supporters
headlines.description: Democrat Xavier Becerra and Republican Steven Hilton addressed supporters as voters took to the polls in California’s governor primary.

headlines.title: Karen Bass to advance in L.A. mayoral runoff race, NBC News projects
headlines.description: NBC News projects incumbent Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass will advance to a November runoff election, with challengers Spencer Pratt and Nithya Raman competing for the other spot. NBC Los Angeles’ Alex Rozier reports from Bass’ campaign headquarters where she spoke ahead of the call.

headlines.title: MAHA-backed Zach Lahn defeats Trump-backed Randy Feenstra in Iowa GOP primary for governor
headlines.description: Rep. Randy Feenstra conceded to businessman Zach Lahn in Iowa’s Republican primary for governor as party voters rejected President Donald Trump’s late endorsement in the race.

headlines.title: Rubio testifies at House hearing
headlines.description: Watch live coverage as Secretary of State Marco Rubio testifies before the House Foreign Affairs Committee.

headlines.title: California standoff suspect is dead and hostages are freed
headlines.description: A man who took several people hostage at a California bank building is dead and all hostages have been freed, police said Wednesday following a roughly 15-hour standoff

headlines.title: Ukraine Hits St. Petersburg With Massive Wave of Drones
headlines.description: A wave of Ukrainian drones targeted sites in St. Petersburg where Russian President Vladimir Putin is kicking off a closely-watched international summit. It comes after Ukraine suffered one of the worst onslaughts of the war. NBC’s Keir Simmons reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: '60 Minutes' Correspondent Scott Pelley Fired From CBS
headlines.description: Veteran “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley has been terminated just one day after a fiery meeting with leadership. Pelley had clashed with the show’s controversial new leader, technology journalist and documentary maker Nick Bilton, attacking his qualifications. Pelley has also been critical of CBS News chief Bari Weiss. NBC’s Erin McLaughlin reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: Elon Musk is set to become the world’s first trillionaire. Democrats are taking aim.
headlines.description: Democrats are sharpening their attacks against Elon Musk, zeroing in on his soon-to-be-trillionaire status before his company SpaceX’s initial public offering.

headlines.title: Supreme Court allows Alabama to use congressional map that eliminates a majority-Black district
headlines.description: The Supreme Court allowed Alabama to use a congressional map that eliminates one of two majority-Black districts in the state in a win for Republicans.

headlines.title: No people, just sheep: The unlikely job that Chinese college grads clamored for
headlines.description: An ad for a shepherding job went viral in China, drawing hundreds of applications from young people eager to escape the stresses of urban life.

headlines.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
headlines.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

headlines.title: Where the redistricting fight stands heading into the midterms
headlines.description: After 10 states enacted new congressional maps, Republicans are in position to gain up to 16 seats this fall, compared to six for the Democrats, as the parties vie for control of the House.
